FAQs
Can I start from $5/d?
Yes. However you need a good number of impressions to be confident in the numbers. I
aim for 1000 impressions before making a decision and ideally 3-5K before I call it a
winner, knowing that the results are stable. These are only benchmark ranges. Also, aim
to give your ads at least 24 hours, if not 48 hours to avoid any false positives or chance
performance
What's the ideal budget?
I'd start with at least $10 per day per ad set, up to $25 per day per ad set.
Do I set an ad set or campaign budget?
Either options work. If you set a campaign budget of $100 per day with 10 ad sets, then
Facebook will decide, based on performance, which ad set to give the budget to. It's a
good way of focussing budget on best performance. However, for testing purposes,
setting budgets at ad set level and monitoring gives you more control to ensure the ad
sets get sufficient spend to give you insights, which is my preference
How do I call a winner?
I'm looking for CTR and CPC as my lead indicators for non video ads. For video I'm
looking at cost per 10 second view (ideally kept below $0.10) and % watched at 25% and
how many get to 10 seconds.
Do I have to run testing in PPE only? Can I test in other Objectives?
I prefer using PPE campaigns because of how Facebook will prioritise those that are
likely to engage with ads from your audience, which is easier for the algorithm to find
than people that are likely to convert. It allow for smaller spends to go in and faster
results to come out from testing.
However there is nothing stopping you from testing with other objectives. For example
with Lead Gen and Mobile App Install Objectives, because they are, to some extent more
similar to PPE than Website Conversion, testing using these objectives can work as well
as running PPE.
The only change therefore is creating a separate Lead Gen or MAI campaign purely for
testing purposes.
I'm testing interests. Do I separate them? Or combine?
When testing interests, I prefer to separate them into their own ad sets so I can isolate
the ones that are working or not. Check out the demo video to see my setup. Once I've
qualified them through testing I'll move into my main campaign and combine interests
into a single ad set if they're similar interests and requiring similar ads.
What if my Graduation Framework test doesn't generate sales/leads?
It's not intended to. Remember, all you are testing is whether an audience reacts
positively to an ad, measured in CTR and CPC. There are indicator metrics further down
the funnel you can analyse which are covered in the demo video.
I'm testing lookalikes (LLAs). Once tested do I keep them separate?
When testing LLAs keep them separate for Graduation Framework testing. So you might
have a 1% of all visitors, view content, add to cart etc - keep them all separate through
testing so you can see which ones work. When moving them to your main campaign,
then it's ok to stack the 1% audiences together. The reason for this is that if you have a
group of high performing 1% audiences, then a) you're potentially making the audience
size bigger, which works better with Facebook generally and b) you're squeezing the
best of all audiences and giving more juicy data to work with for the algorithm.
If however you're running a nested test, that is a 1%, 1-2%, 2-3% of a single custom
audience then again it is possible to combine, if you need larger audiences. If the 1%
(which for the US is over 1M generally) is working and so is the 2-3% for example then
keeping them separate allows you to manage your budget across the ad sets, increasing
and decreasing as you see fit. If you need a larger audience then merging them is fine
(for example in the UK where 1% is around 400K).
